title: pittosporum undulatum
intro: Pittosporum undulatum, also known as the Miro or New Zealand Christmas tree, is a small to medium-sized evergreen tree native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Pittosporum undulatum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include a tincture, made by soaking the leaves and twigs in a solvent, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and a topical cream, applied directly to the skin to aid in wound healing.
